incidents.actual_root_cause: Deployment of v2.4.1 introduced an N+1 SQL query in the order lookup function. Under production load, each API request now executes 47 queries instead of 1, exhausting the database connection pool after ~12 minutes of traffic.

recent_deployments.commit_message: feat: add order history to payment confirmation page

recent_deployments._design_note: RED HERRING: This deploy happened 17 min before DEP-8821. Notification service is healthy. Agent must not blame this.

logs.message: Deployment DEP-8821 completed. New version v2.4.1 is live.
Health check passed. Serving traffic normally.
DB connection pool utilization at 78%. Threshold is 80%.
Slow query detected: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE user_id=? took 1842ms
TimeoutError: Could not acquire DB connection from pool after 5000ms. Pool size: 20, Active: 20, Waiting: 47
HTTP 500: Payment confirmation failed for order ORD-994821. Root: DB_POOL_EXHAUSTED
HTTP 500: Payment confirmation failed for order ORD-994834. Root: DB_POOL_EXHAUSTED
CRITICAL: Error rate at 43%. Alert threshold breached.
Query trace for /confirm endpoint: executed 47 SELECT queries for single payment_id=PP-18821
logs._design_note: SMOKING GUN: 47 queries for 1 request = N+1 bug. Agent should find this.

metrics._red_herring_cpu: CPU is elevated (71%) but NOT the cause. It's a symptom of doing 47x more DB work per request.
metrics._red_herring_memory: Memory is fine (58%). Agent must not hallucinate a memory issue.

incidents.actual_root_cause: auth-service v3.1.0 introduced a memory leak: JWT blacklist entries are stored in a dict that is never cleaned up. Under sustained load, the process exhausted all available heap memory over 4 hours and triggered OOM kills.

recent_deployments.commit_message: security: add JWT token revocation blacklist
recent_deployments._design_note: 4 HOUR GAP before errors. Hard because the temporal distance makes the deployment look less suspicious.

logs.message: Deployment v3.1.0 live. JWT blacklist feature enabled.
JWT blacklist initialized. Current entries: 0
Heap memory at 65%. JWT blacklist entries: 84,219
Heap memory at 81%. GC pressure increasing. JWT blacklist entries: 189,441
java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space at com.company.auth.BlacklistCache.add()
Pod auth-service-7d8f9c-xkp2m killed by OOM killer. Restarting...
Pod restarted but memory growing again immediately. BlacklistCache.size()=0 on restart, adding entries at 420/min
Authentication failures spiking. Downstream services returning 401.

metrics._design_note: HARD: 4-hour gap, no error_code pointing to deployment. Must reason: OOM + blacklist entries growing = unbounded cache = new feature = deployment.

incidents.actual_root_cause: Deployment v1.2.0 removed a null-check for `user.preferences` in the email template renderer. When any user with no saved preferences triggers a notification, the service crashes with NullPointerException immediately.

recent_deployments.commit_message: feat: personalized email templates using user preferences
recent_deployments._design_note: 3-MINUTE GAP. Extremely obvious. Version deployed, crashes start 3 min later. Easy test case.

logs.message: Deployment DEP-8901 successful. v1.2.0 is live.
NullPointerException at email_renderer.py:line 84 in render_personalized(). user.preferences is None.
NullPointerException at email_renderer.py:line 84 in render_personalized(). user.preferences is None.
NullPointerException at email_renderer.py:line 84 in render_personalized(). user.preferences is None.
Error rate: 100%. All notification sends failing. Alerting on-call.

incidents.actual_root_cause: recommendation-engine v4.0.0 upgraded numpy from 1.24 to 2.0. The new numpy changed default integer overflow behavior, causing the collaborative filtering model to produce NaN scores for ~30% of user segments, which maps to 500 errors.

recent_deployments.commit_message: chore: upgrade numpy to 2.0, scikit-learn to 1.4
perf: increase upstream timeout from 10s to 15s
recent_deployments._design_note: RED HERRING: api-gateway deployed 25 min before. The timeout change means latency metrics look inflated. Agent must not blame api-gateway.

logs.message: ValueError: cannot convert float NaN to integer at collab_filter.py:line 211 in score_segment()
DeprecationWarning: numpy integer overflow behavior changed in 2.0. Use explicit dtype casting. (This was previously silently truncated)
Recommendation fetch failed for user_segment=EU_PREMIUM. NaN propagated through scoring pipeline.
Upstream recommendation-engine returning 500 for 31% of requests. Timeout set to 15s per DEP-8920.
logs._design_note: RED HERRING: api-gateway logs look suspicious but it's innocent.

metrics._red_herring_latency: High latency (14.2s) is partly because api-gateway timeout was increased to 15s — requests hang longer before failing. Not the root cause.

incidents.actual_root_cause: order-service v5.3.0 included a feature flag `ENABLE_REAL_TIME_INVENTORY_CHECK` that was accidentally set to `true` in production config. This new code path calls inventory-service synchronously for every item in the cart, but inventory-service has no SLA for synchronous calls, causing 8-12 second latency per order.

recent_deployments.commit_message: feat: real-time inventory check (behind feature flag, off by default)

logs.message: Feature flag ENABLE_REAL_TIME_INVENTORY_CHECK is TRUE. Real-time sync calls enabled.
Unexpected synchronous call pattern detected. Caller: order-service. This endpoint is designed for async use only.
Checkout timed out for cart CART-88421 (18 items). inventory-service sync call: 11,204ms. Threshold: 10,000ms.
Thread pool utilization at 89%. Requests queuing due to slow inventory-service calls.
Incoming request rate from order-service increased 40x in last 10 minutes. Not designed for this load pattern.
logs._design_note: Hard: inventory-service is being hammered. Agent might incorrectly blame inventory-service. Must trace back to feature flag.

metrics._red_herring: inventory-service metrics look terrible. CPU 78%, latency 9s. But it's a VICTIM, not the cause. Agent must identify the feature flag as root cause.

recent_deployments._design_note: RED HERRING: This is an automated patch deploy. The memory leak exists in code before this version. Agent must identify gradual heap growth, not deployment.

logs.message: Service restarted after deployment. Heap: 1.2GB / 8GB
Heap: 2.6GB / 8GB. Normal operation.
Heap: 4.8GB / 8GB. GC runs increasing. Possible memory leak.
Heap: 7.1GB / 8GB. GC pause times: avg 2.1s. Service degrading.
GC overhead limit exceeded. Heap: 7.9GB / 8GB. User session lookups failing.
OutOfMemoryError. Pod user-service-abc123 killed. Session cache contained 2.3M live UserSession objects.

metrics._pattern: Memory grows linearly over 6 hours. Classic leak pattern. Deploy 6 hours ago is a red herring.

incidents.actual_root_cause: The report-generator service runs daily analytics queries that take 45-90 minutes. At peak volume, 8 concurrent reports each held a DB connection for the full duration, exhausting the connection pool (size: 10) and blocking all other operations.

logs.message: Scheduled report generation started. Jobs queued: 8
DB connections acquired: 8/10. Running report jobs.
DB connection pool exhausted. 2 remaining connections held by long-running report queries (>70 min).
API request failed: Cannot acquire DB connection. Pool size 10, all in use by background reports.
report-generator returning 503. Affected endpoints: /api/v1/reports/*, /api/v1/dashboard/*

metrics._no_deployment: No recent deployment. Resource exhaustion from scheduled batch job.

incidents.actual_root_cause: The media-service has unrotated access logs filling the /var/log partition. At 100% disk usage, the application cannot write new logs or temp files, causing all video upload operations to fail with a generic IOError.

logs.message: Disk usage on /var/log: 91%. Log rotation has not run in 14 days.
Disk usage on /var/log: 97%. Approaching critical threshold.
IOError: No space left on device. Failed to write temp file for video upload job-99821.
IOError: No space left on device. Failed to write temp file for video upload job-99822.
All video upload operations failing. Error: filesystem is read-only due to disk full.

incidents.actual_root_cause: Stripe's payment gateway experienced a regional outage in ap-south-1 (Mumbai). All payment processing requests from checkout-service to Stripe's API are returning 503. The issue is entirely external — no internal code change caused this.

recent_deployments.commit_message: fix: improve retry logic for payment API calls
recent_deployments._design_note: RED HERRING: Deploy touched stripe_client.py 4 hours ago. Agent might incorrectly blame this. Key signal: external Stripe status page shows outage.

logs.message: Stripe API returned 503 Service Unavailable. Endpoint: https://api.stripe.com/v1/payment_intents. Response: {error: {type: api_error, message: 'We are having trouble with our servers in the ap-south-1 region'}}
Stripe API returned 503. Retry 1/3 failed.
Stripe API returned 503. Retry 2/3 failed. Giving up.
Payment processing unavailable. All Stripe API calls failing. Incident suspected on Stripe side. Recommend checking https://status.stripe.com
No internal errors. Application logic is healthy. All failures originating from external Stripe API.

metrics._external_outage_indicator: Internal latency (210ms) is completely healthy. All errors come from external Stripe API calls.

incidents.actual_root_cause: The shipping-service hit FedEx API rate limits (1000 req/min). A new marketing campaign drove 8x the normal order volume. FedEx returns HTTP 429, which the shipping-service incorrectly handles as a generic 500, making it look like an internal failure.

logs.message: Order volume increasing. Processing 820 shipment requests/minute (baseline: 100/min)
FedEx API error. HTTP 429 Too Many Requests. Retry-After: 60s. X-RateLimit-Remaining: 0
UnhandledHTTPError: 429 mapped to generic InternalServiceError. Shipment label generation failed for ORDER-771221.
logs._design_note: Hard: The 429 is not properly logged as rate-limit. It shows as INTERNAL_ERROR. Agent must dig deep to find FEDEX_RATE_LIMIT.

logs.message: Flash sale campaign 'SUMMER2024' launched. Traffic surge expected.

metrics._hard_clue: fedex_api_429_responses is the smoking gun. But it's buried in metrics.

incidents.actual_root_cause: SendGrid's email delivery API started returning HTTP 503 with a 45-second timeout. The analytics-service queues email reports and all jobs are hanging waiting for SendGrid, causing the job queue to fill up.

logs.message: SendGrid API timeout after 45000ms. Endpoint: https://api.sendgrid.com/v3/mail/send. Job: weekly-report-batch-4421
Email job queue filling up. 47 jobs waiting. Workers stuck on SendGrid calls.
Job queue at maximum capacity (100 jobs). New jobs being rejected.

incidents.actual_root_cause: Redis cache cluster ran out of memory (maxmemory policy: noeviction). All cache SET operations began failing. Without cache, services fell back to the database. Sudden 10x DB load caused connection pool exhaustion across 4 services simultaneously, creating a platform-wide outage.

recent_deployments.commit_message: feat: cache product catalog in Redis with 1-hour TTL
recent_deployments._design_note: This deployment WORSENED the problem by adding more data to an already-full Redis. But it did not CAUSE it. Root cause is Redis maxmemory policy. This is a hard cascade.

logs.message: Memory usage at 98%. maxmemory=16GB, used=15.7GB. Policy: noeviction.
OOM command not allowed when 'maxmemory' is reached. Redis SET failed for key product:catalog:v2
Redis cache unavailable. Falling back to PostgreSQL for all product lookups.
Redis cache unavailable. Falling back to PostgreSQL for session lookups.
Connection pool exhausted. Active: 200/200. Waiting: 847. Multiple services calling DB directly.
Cannot acquire DB connection. Platform-wide DB pool saturation.
Cannot acquire DB connection. Platform-wide DB pool saturation.

incidents.actual_root_cause: inventory-service began running a slow database migration query (alter table adding index on 50M rows) which locked the inventory table. checkout-service calls to inventory began timing out at 30s. This filled checkout's thread pool, causing checkout to stop responding. order-service, which calls checkout, then also became unresponsive — cascading failure across 3 services.

logs.message: Running DB migration: ALTER TABLE inventory ADD INDEX idx_sku_warehouse (sku_id, warehouse_id). Estimated time: 45 min.
Table lock on `inventory` detected during migration. Read queries may be slow.
inventory-service response time degrading. P99 at 18,000ms. Threshold: 5,000ms.
Thread pool at 95% utilization. 180 threads blocked waiting for inventory-service responses.
Thread pool exhausted. Rejecting new checkout requests. HTTP 503 returned.
checkout-service returning 503. Order placement failing. Cascading from checkout outage.

metrics._cascade_pattern: Root: DB migration lock -> inventory slow -> checkout threads blocked -> checkout down -> order-service down

incidents.actual_root_cause: FALSE ALARM. A YouTube tech influencer posted a review of the product, driving 15x normal traffic for 20 minutes. Metrics spiked (CPU, latency) but auto-scaling handled the load. No errors, no failures. Alert triggered on CPU threshold but system was healthy.

logs.message: Traffic spike detected. Requests/sec: 8,200 (baseline: 540). Initiating auto-scale.
Auto-scaling triggered. Scaling from 3 to 12 pods.
12 pods running. Traffic normalized. Error rate: 0.02%
Referral traffic source: youtube.com/watch?v=xyz. Organic viral traffic.
Traffic returning to baseline. Scaling back to 3 pods.

metrics._false_alarm: CPU hit 88% but error rate is 0.02%. System self-healed. No action required.

incidents.actual_root_cause: FALSE ALARM. The nightly batch-processor job runs at 03:00 AM UTC and processes 2M records. For 45 minutes, it consumes 70% CPU and elevates DB read latency. Monitoring alerts fire every night but the job always completes successfully. This is expected behavior.

logs.message: Nightly batch job started. Records to process: 2,143,882.
ALERT: batch-processor CPU at 71%. Threshold: 70%. Auto-generated alert.
ALERT: DB read latency elevated to 2,200ms (baseline: 120ms).
Progress: 1,200,000 / 2,143,882 records. Estimated completion: 03:45 UTC.
Nightly batch complete. 2,143,882 records processed. 0 errors. Duration: 47 minutes.
RESOLVED: CPU and DB latency returned to baseline. Batch job completed successfully.

metrics._expected_behavior: This fires every single night. Should be excluded from incident tracking or alert threshold should be raised.
